dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8550 IH: To allow States to require payment of State fees related to boating as a condition for issuance of a vessel number and to collect such fees in conjunction with other fees related to vessel numbering.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia (for herself, Mr. Moore of Utah, and Ms. Balint) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To allow States to require payment of State fees related to boating as a condition for issuance of a vessel number and to collect such fees in conjunction with other fees related to vessel numbering.

section.header: Collection of State fees related to boating
section.text: Section 12307 of title 46, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking The authority and inserting (a) In general.—The authority; and
by striking that are— and inserting that—;
by inserting are before prescribed; and
by striking ; or and inserting a semicolon;
by inserting are before related; and
by striking the period and inserting ; or; and

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:
require the payment of State fees related to boating, which may include fees for search and rescue operations, boating safety measures, or efforts to address aquatic invasive species.

subsection.text: A State issuing authority may collect a fee described in subsection (a)(3) in conjunction with the collection of any other fee established under this section.

Fees collected by States under the authority this section may only be used to fund activities directly related to improving recreational boating, boater safety, boater access, use of waterways by recreational boaters, and aquatic invasive species.
